sections.paragraph:
φαιλόνης, ου, ὁ is to be spelled so (B-D-F §25) as against the great uncials and critical editions, which have φελόνης (PFay 347 [II a.d.]). This appears to be a Lat. loanw. (paenula; see Hahn p. 10, 8; EFraenkel, ZVS 42, 1909, 115, 1; ESchwyzer, MusHelv 3, ’46, 50–52; but s. B. below), also in rabb. in var. spellings. Its original form was φαινόλας (Rhinthon [III b.c.], Com. Graec. Fgm. 7 Kaibel [in Pollux 7, 61]) or φαινόλης (Epict. 4, 8, 34; Artem. 2, 3; 5, 29; Athen. 3, 97e; POxy 736, 4; 1737, 9; 15; PGiss 10, 21; PHamb 10, 19 [II b.c.]; Gignac I 100), also φαινόλιον (POxy 531, 14 [II a.d.]; 936, 18; 19). From these by metathesis (s. CLobeck, Pathologiae Sermonis Graeci Elementa I 1853, 514; W-S. §5, 18; B-D-F §32, 2; Mlt-H. 81; 106; 155) came φαιλόνης (which is still quotable at least in its dim. form φαιλόνιον [-ώνιον]: POxy 933, 30; PGiss 12, 4 [II a.d.]; BGU 816, 24 [III a.d.]; cp. Mod. Gk. φελόνι) cloak (POxy 531, 14 τὰ ἱμάτια τὰ λευκὰ τὰ δυνάμενα μετὰ τῶν πορφυρῶν φορεῖσθαι φαινολίων. Likew. Epict.; Athen., loc. cit. In view of these pass. the translation ‘valise’ is excluded; s. Field, Notes 217f, cited in M-M; also excluded is the interpretation in the direction of διφθέρα, the leather cover for papyrus rolls) 2 Ti 4:13 (on the subject-matter s. POxy 1489 [III a.d.] τὸ κιθώνιν [=χιτώνιον] ἐπιλέλησμαι παρὰ Τεκοῦσαν εἰς τὸν πυλῶνα. πέμψον μοι).—See B. 417, where φαινόλα is treated as the original fr. which Lat. paenula is borrowed, and not vice versa; s. also Mlt-H. 106.—Frisk. DELG s.v. φαίνω A. M-M.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Lat. Lat. = Latin

loanw. loanw. = loanword

Hahn Hahn = LH., Rom und Romanismus im griechischen-römischen Osten—List 6

ZVS ZVS = Zeitschrift für vergleichende Sprachforschung—List 6

MusHelv MusHelv = Museum Helveticum—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

var. var. = various(ly)

Rhinthon Rhinthon , III b.c.—List 5

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

PHamb PHamb = Griechische Papyrusurkunden der Hamburger Staats- und Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

Gignac Gignac = FG., Grammar of Greek Papyri—Lists 4, 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

dim. dim. = diminutive

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Mod. Gk. Mod. Gk. = Modern Greek

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Field, Notes Field, Notes = Notes on the Translation of the NT

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

  1. fr. = from

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1046
